methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ETH custody in Bridge (all msg.value retained until processed/refunded/recall paths)
ETH custody in ERC20Vault/ERC721Vault/ERC1155Vault (temporary msg.value during onMessageInvocation/onMessageRecalled flows)
Canonical ERC20 balances held by ERC20Vault (deposited via safeTransferFrom)
Canonical ERC721 tokens held by ERC721Vault (deposited via safeTransferFrom)
Canonical ERC1155 tokens held by ERC1155Vault (deposited via safeBatchTransferFrom)
Bridged ERC20 supply (mint/burn) in BridgedERC20 / USDCAdapter
Bridged token migration value flows (BridgedERC20Base burn() can trigger mint() on migratingAddress)
Fee mechanism: message.fee paid to message processor (Bridge.processMessage) and refunded to refundTo/destOwner
state_assets: Bridge.proofReceipt[msgHash] (receivedAt, preferredExecutor) used for delay/executor preference and suspension
Bridge.addressBanned[addr] (ban list for message invocation targets)
Bridge.__ctx / transient context slot _CTX_SLOT (msgHash/from/srcChainId) for downstream vault authorization
SignalService.topBlockId[chainId][kind] (latest synced block id per chain/kind)
SignalService signals stored via getSignalSlot(chainId, app, signal) => value (raw storage mapping via sstore/sload)
ERC20Vault.btokenBlacklist[btoken] (blacklist old bridged tokens on migration)
BaseNFTVault.bridgedToCanonical / canonicalToBridged mappings for ERC721/ERC1155 vaults
BridgedERC20.srcToken, srcChainId, snapshooter, __srcDecimals (bridged token metadata/governance state)
EssentialContract pause state (__paused) and reentrancy lock (__reentry or transient lock)
privilege_assets: Owner (Ownable2Step) across all EssentialContract-derived contracts (upgrade/pause/admin operations)
Pause authority: EssentialContract.pause/unpause gated by _authorizePause (varies per contract)
Bridge watchdog role via AddressResolver name: bridge_watchdog (suspendMessages, banAddress)
Bridge pauser role via AddressResolver name: bridge_pauser (pause/unpause authorization)
SignalService: owner can authorize() addresses; pause is disabled (reverts)
Vault owners (onlyOwner) can change bridged token mapping (ERC20Vault.changeBridgedToken)
Bridged token mint/burn authorization by named vaults (onlyFromNamed("erc20_vault"/"erc721_vault"/"erc1155_vault"))
BridgedERC20 snapshooter privilege (snapshot creation and vote checkpointing impact)
AddressResolver-configured named addresses (bridge, signal_service, vault names, bridged token implementations) as implicit trust anchors
reputation_assets: ERC20Votes voting power in BridgedERC20 (delegation, checkpoints, snapshots) affecting governance weight if used
SignalService chain data cache integrity (topBlockId, cached roots) as trust basis for cross-chain proof acceptance
Bridge message lifecycle integrity (status transitions and context correctness) affecting protocol trust

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: EssentialContract (UUPSUpgradeable + Ownable2StepUpgradeable + AddressResolver + custom pause/nonReentrant) is the base for Bridge, SignalService, BaseVault, BridgedERC20Base, BridgedERC20, BridgedERC721, BridgedERC1155, USDCAdapter. Vaults inherit BaseVault (and BaseNFTVault for NFT vaults). BridgedERC20 extends BridgedERC20Base and OpenZeppelin ERC20SnapshotUpgradeable + ERC20VotesUpgradeable. BridgedERC721 extends EssentialContract + ERC721Upgradeable. BridgedERC1155 extends EssentialContract + ERC1155Upgradeable + IERC1155MetadataURIUpgradeable. ERC1155Vault also inherits ERC1155ReceiverUpgradeable; ERC721Vault implements IERC721Receiver.
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin: Address, SafeERC20, IERC20/IERC20Metadata, ERC1967Proxy, UUPSUpgradeable, Ownable2StepUpgradeable, ERC20SnapshotUpgradeable, ERC20VotesUpgradeable, ERC20Permit, ERC721Upgradeable, ERC1155Upgradeable, ERC1155ReceiverUpgradeable, IERC165Upgradeable
Custom: AddressResolver (not included), LibTrieProof, LibAddress, LibBridgedToken
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: UUPS upgradeability via EssentialContract (inherits UUPSUpgradeable) for all EssentialContract-derived contracts. Additionally, bridged token instances are deployed behind ERC1967Proxy pointing to named implementations ("bridged_erc20", "bridged_erc721", "bridged_erc1155"). No Diamond pattern present.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Owner-centric (Ownable2Step) plus AddressResolver-based named roles using onlyFromOwnerOrNamed(name) and onlyFromNamed(name). Bridge pause authorization is delegated to owner or resolved "bridge_pauser"; watchdog functions to owner or resolved "bridge_watchdog". SignalService sync authorization via isAuthorized mapping managed by owner; pausing is disabled. Vault callbacks (onMessageInvocation/onMessageRecalled) authenticate origin via onlyFromBridge + Bridge.context() checks. Bridged token mint/burn restricted to corresponding vault names (except BridgedERC20Base migration behaviors).

state_changes: proofReceipt[msgHash].receivedAt (set to max uint64 for suspend or current timestamp for unsuspend)
proofReceipt[msgHash] (write receivedAt; delete on recall execution)

external_calls: ETH refunds via LibAddress.sendEther to msg.sender and refundTo/destOwner
ISignalService.sendSignal(failureSignal) if status becomes FAILED (via _updateMessageStatus, though FAILED not set here)

state_changes: arbitrary storage slot derived from (chainid,msg.sender,_signal) via sstore
may cache chain data by calling _syncChainData (writes signal slots and topBlockId) depending on CacheOption in proof
external bridged token migration flags (via changeMigrationStatus on old/new)
ERC20Vault token balances (canonical deposits) or bridged token supply (burn)
may deploy new ERC1967Proxy bridged token and update bridgedToCanonical/canonicalToBridged
ERC20Vault token balances (canonical withdrawals) or bridged token supply (mint)
external_calls: IERC20.safeTransfer(to, amount) if canonical on this chain

state_changes: ERC20Vault token balances (canonical withdrawals back to srcOwner) or bridged token supply (mint back)
external_calls: IBridge(msg.sender).context() via checkRecallMessageContext() (requires msg.sender==bridge and ctx.from==bridge)

state_changes: ERC721Vault custody of canonical ERC721 or bridged supply burns
may deploy new ERC1967Proxy bridged token and update bridgedToCanonical/canonicalToBridged
ERC721Vault custody changes (canonical withdrawals) or bridged ERC721 mints
ERC721Vault custody changes (canonical withdrawals back) or bridged ERC721 mints back
ERC1155Vault custody of canonical ERC1155 or bridged supply burns
may deploy new ERC1967Proxy bridged token and update bridgedToCanonical/canonicalToBridged
external_calls: IERC1155.safeTransferFrom(user->vault, id, amount) in loop (canonical path)
BridgedERC1155(_op.token).burn(user, id, amount) in loop (bridged path)

state_changes: ERC1155Vault custody changes (canonical withdrawals) or bridged ERC1155 mintBatch
external_calls: IERC1155.safeBatchTransferFrom(vault->to, ids, amts) or BridgedERC1155(token).mintBatch(to, ids, amts)

state_changes: ERC1155Vault custody changes (canonical withdrawals back) or bridged ERC1155 mintBatch back
external_calls: IERC1155.safeBatchTransferFrom(vault->srcOwner, ids, amts) or BridgedERC1155(token).mintBatch(srcOwner, ids, amts)
_burnToken (implementation-specific, e.g., ERC20 _burn or USDC burn flow)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Inherits UUPSUpgradeable via EssentialContract; upgrade authorized by onlyOwner in _authorizeUpgrade. Uses transient storage for context/reentrancy on chainid==1.
Pause intentionally unsupported (_authorizePause reverts), but pause/unpause functions are inherited and callable; they will revert. Upgrade authorized by onlyOwner.
Abstract, but deployed implementations (ERC20Vault/ERC721Vault/ERC1155Vault) inherit UUPS and expose pause/unpause publicly.
Also deploys new ERC1967Proxy instances for bridged tokens using named implementation "bridged_erc20".
Also deploys new ERC1967Proxy instances for bridged tokens using named implementation "bridged_erc721".
Also deploys new ERC1967Proxy instances for bridged tokens using named implementation "bridged_erc1155".
Upgradeable ERC20 with snapshots/votes; special care for storage layout across multiple OZ upgradeable mixins.
Abstract but upgradeable base; real deployments use BridgedERC20 or USDCAdapter behind ERC1967Proxy.
Adapter relies on external USDC mint/burn/transferFrom; inherits BridgedERC20Base authorization and migration logic.
Mint/burn restricted to named vault; pause/unpause publicly callable and authorized by onlyOwner (EssentialContract default).
Mint/burn restricted to named vault; pause/unpause publicly callable and authorized by onlyOwner (EssentialContract default).
